items.title: Introducing Telegram as a New Chat Channel in Coding Bridge 🚀
items.summary: This update integrates Telegram as a first-class chat channel alongside WeChat, enhancing the channels portal. Key changes include the addition of a Telegram adapter, a new async Bot API client, and comprehensive configuration options, all while ensuring security measures like token protection. The implementation allows for simultaneous operation of both channels, streamlining user interactions.

items.title: Enhance Blogger Skill to Publish Live by Default in Skills 🚀
items.summary: This update modifies the Blogger skill to publish articles live by default when users request to publish. Previously, articles were created as drafts, leading to confusion. Key changes include improved error handling for unregistered callers and empty blog lists, ensuring a smoother user experience.

items.title: Introducing Opt-in Live Tool Approvals in Coding Bridge 🚀
items.summary: This update adds an opt-in feature for live tool approvals via the channels portal. When enabled, any tool requests made by an agent will be held for user approval instead of executing automatically. The implementation includes a file-backed approval store and has been thoroughly tested to ensure no regression for existing setups.

items.title: Enhance Telegram Bot Publishing Skill in Skills 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a new skill for publishing to Telegram channels and groups via a bot using the official Bot API. Key features include message sending, editing, and deletion, along with verification of bot permissions. The skill is designed to facilitate broadcasting content efficiently while ensuring proper access control.

items.title: Enhance Telegram Bot Connector for Channel Broadcasting in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces the new `telegrambot/telegrambot` BYOC connector, enabling users to broadcast messages to Telegram channels or groups using a bot token from @BotFather. The connector operates over the official Bot API and requires the bot to be an admin in the channel. Key features include secure token handling and compatibility with existing BYOC structures.

items.title: Set fish-tts as the default TTS engine in Platform Service 🎤
items.summary: This update makes AceData's fish-tts the default text-to-speech engine in the HyperFrames audio engine, ensuring that all maestro videos receive authentic Chinese audio without requiring agent adherence. Key changes include a new build-time script that routes audio requests to fish-tts and a Dockerfile modification to integrate this patch seamlessly. This enhancement addresses previous issues with silent tracks and improves overall audio quality for users.

items.title: Update live Wisdom D4s_v4 fallback strategy in Index 🚀
items.summary: This update records that the live Wisdom AKS production is utilizing the `Standard_D4s_v4` SKU due to a quota limitation in the eastasia region. It maintains `D4s_v5` as the preferred option while confirming `D4s_v4` as a fallback. Additionally, early cost estimates have been adjusted to reflect a range of $170-180/month for both SKUs.

items.title: Enhance Bluesky Connector Instructions for Full Handle Requirement in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update clarifies the instructions for the Bluesky connector by explicitly stating that users must enter their full handle, including the domain, to avoid authentication errors. The `description` and `handle` field help text have been revised to prevent confusion that led to login failures. No changes to schema or logic were made, ensuring no impact on other connectors.

items.title: Fix Bluesky Handle Normalization in Skills 🚀
items.summary: This update addresses an issue where Bluesky posts failed due to the use of bare usernames instead of full handles. The connector now normalizes bare usernames to include the domain `.bsky.social` during session creation, ensuring compatibility with Bluesky's requirements. Custom-domain handles and DIDs remain unaffected, improving user experience and reducing authentication errors.

items.title: Enhance WeChat Onboarding & Speed Up Contact Search in Coding Bridge 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a QR onboarding feature for WeChat, allowing users to log in seamlessly when signed out. Additionally, the contact search functionality has been optimized, reducing initial search times from ~7 seconds to ~1 second through concurrent paging and improved filtering. These enhancements significantly improve user experience and efficiency.

items.title: Renamed K8s Service for Consistency in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update renames the WebExtrator API's K8s service from `webextrator-service` to `platform-service-webextrator`, aligning it with the naming convention of other upstream workers. The change includes a port update from 80 to 8000 while maintaining the same pod selectors, ensuring seamless integration with existing services. The migration was executed with zero downtime and has been verified to function correctly.

items.title: Fix Google Blogger Token Mapping in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update resolves a critical issue where publishing to Blogger from Studio chat failed due to a missing OAuth token mapping. The explicit mapping for the `google/blogger` connector to `GOOGLE_BLOGGER_TOKEN` has been added, ensuring that the skill can correctly access the required token. Validation tests confirm that the changes are functioning as expected.

items.title: Fix Windows Node Path Issues for MCP Servers in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update resolves a critical issue where Windows GUI-launched applications inherited a stale PATH, causing MCP servers to fail when executing bare `node` or `npx` commands. The `resolveEnhancedPath()` function has been enhanced to append standard Node installation directories derived from stable environment variables, ensuring reliable command resolution. New tests have been added to verify the correctness of the path derivation.

items.title: Enhance web tools with internal routing in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update routes the `web_search` and `web_fetch` tools to internal services, improving performance and eliminating external API dependencies. The `web_search` now correctly handles result counts and geo-location, while `web_fetch` utilizes a dedicated WebExtrator for better data extraction. Configuration changes ensure seamless integration with existing services without requiring deployment environment adjustments.

items.title: Enhance extraction retries for thin content in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a fallback mechanism for extraction jobs that encounter thin content after initial extraction. By implementing a retry strategy with a configured hydration delay, the system can improve the visible text length without unnecessarily increasing wait times for all requests. Key changes include the addition of a new function to determine when to retry and ensuring that non-HTML content is not retried.

items.title: Enhance Group Control with Allowed Groups Policy in Coding Bridge 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces the `allowed_groups` policy, allowing for more granular control over which WeChat group chats the bot can respond to. The portal now features a functional group picker, enabling users to easily select allowed groups. This enhancement improves access control, ensuring the bot only interacts in designated team groups while maintaining existing functionality for private messages.

items.title: Fix nano-banana pricing tiers and billing issues in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update resolves critical billing issues for the nano-banana pricing tiers by ensuring distinct 1K, 2K, and 4K options are displayed and billed correctly. The previous implementation had a bug that caused all requests to bill as free due to mismatched resolution literals. Now, the rules have been updated to use lowercase literals, and the pricing page accurately reflects the available tiers.

items.title: Enhance SPA Hydration for Better Content Extraction in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update addresses the thin SPA extraction issue identified in the WebExtrator benchmark across 48 sites. It introduces an adaptive hydration retry mechanism that waits for a short duration if the initial visible text is below a specified threshold, ensuring more robust content retrieval without impacting normal long pages. Key configuration options for hydration timing and thresholds have also been documented.

items.title: Enhance nano-banana skill with official model variants in Skills 🚀
items.summary: This update syncs the `nano-banana-image` skill with the latest upstream Docs commit, introducing three new `:official` model variants: `nano-banana:official`, `nano-banana-2:official`, and `nano-banana-pro:official`. The documentation has been updated to reflect these changes, enhancing clarity for users on available models and their parameters.

items.title: New VK Skill for Posting and Managing Content in Skills 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a new VK skill that allows users to publish posts to their VK profile or community wall, as well as retrieve and manage their recent posts using the official VK API. The skill includes functionality for posting, listing, and deleting posts, specifically tailored for the Russian-speaking audience. Validation has confirmed that all methods and parameters align with VK's API specifications.

items.title: Enhance Russian Market Reach with VK & Habr Connectors in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces two new connectors for the Russian market: VK (ВКонтакте) and Habr. The VK connector utilizes a BYOC access token for posting, while the Habr connector operates as a cookie-BYOC due to the absence of an official write API. These additions significantly enhance content distribution capabilities for Russian-speaking users.

items.title: Enhance 创收联盟 Page with New Images in Backend 🎨
items.summary: This update adds two AI-generated marketing images to the 创收联盟 long-form article, enhancing visual appeal. A hero banner and a commission-ladder illustration have been incorporated without altering the existing content. The images were verified for safety and are hosted on a stable CDN.

items.title: Introducing the AKS Appliance Workload Provider in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update adds the new `aks_appliance` workload provider, enabling rapid deployment of the Wisdom WeChat-bot service on Azure AKS using a golden snapshot. The provider significantly reduces setup time from ~40 minutes to approximately 7 minutes by utilizing a CoW clone of a pre-built Windows VM. Key enhancements include new deployment utilities and comprehensive testing to ensure reliability.

items.title: Enhance HappyHorse Pricing Display Rules in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update improves the HappyHorse service pricing display by ensuring only clean public options are shown. The visible options now include `720P` and `1080P`, while a hidden fallback rule is retained for compatibility. Validation tests confirm the expected behavior with all relevant checks passing successfully.

items.title: Enhance Service Cost Display Rules in Index 🚀
items.summary: This update clarifies the rules for service display cost files in the `create-service` workflow. It emphasizes that public pricing should not replicate Gateway billing rules, ensuring a cleaner user experience. Additionally, it introduces focused testing requirements when there are discrepancies between API billing costs and service display costs.

items.title: Enhance CLI with Official Model Variants in Clis 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces three new official model variants to the CLI, aligning with the latest OpenAPI spec for `nano-banana`. The changes include updates to the model choices in `core/output.py` and enhancements to the test suite to ensure all models are properly validated. Users can now utilize these official variants when generating images, improving flexibility and options.

items.title: New Official Nano-Banana Models Pricing in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces pricing for three new `:official` nano-banana models on the `/nano-banana/images` API, ensuring a 40% gross margin. Key changes include updated billing rules, display pricing, and OpenAPI model enum additions. Documentation has been enhanced to reflect these new models, and tests confirm the accuracy of pricing across all tiers.

items.title: Upgrade checkout action to version 7 in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update bumps the actions/checkout from version 6 to 7, enhancing the workflow's functionality. Key changes include improved handling of fork pull requests and updates to several dependencies, ensuring better performance and security. The modifications affect CI and other workflows that utilize the checkout action.

items.title: Update Tencent Cloud SDK to Latest Version in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update bumps the `tencentcloud-sdk-python` package from version 3.1.110 to 3.1.119 in the Python dependencies. The change ensures compatibility with newer Python versions and includes various improvements and fixes. Developers are encouraged to upgrade their Python version as support for `python v3.9` will be discontinued.

items.title: Enhance HappyHorse Service Presentation in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update improves the HappyHorse service by backfilling asset URLs and enhancing the service introduction with a full HTML landing page. It replaces low-quality examples with verified CDN assets and addresses a billing edge case for resolution matching. The official pricing has been set to reflect upstream costs while maintaining public billing rates.

items.title: Enhance Marketing Material Generation in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update refines the auto-generation of marketing materials by implementing a stronger model and raising the quality score threshold to 88 across all channels. The 'x' channel has been retired due to consistently low performance, and a mandatory call-to-action has been added to every prompt. These changes aim to ensure only high-quality materials are published, enhancing overall marketing effectiveness.

items.title: Enhance Marketing Material Tools in M C Ps 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces four new read-only tools to the AceDataCloud MCP server for managing the PublishMaterial marketing-copy catalog. Key features include searching materials by various criteria, picking random materials, listing publish channels, and retrieving detailed material information. These enhancements improve the efficiency of accessing marketing content for users.

items.title: Restored WordPress Logo in Backend 🎨
items.summary: The broken WordPress connector logo URL has been replaced with a verified SVG asset from the AceDataCloud CDN. Validation confirmed that the new URL returns a successful response, ensuring the logo displays correctly. This update enhances the visual consistency of the application.

items.title: Update DiDi Logo in Backend 🎨
items.summary: Replaced the placeholder logo for the DiDi connector with the official logo from the CDN. The same logo is now used for both `icon_url` and `publisher_logo_url`, ensuring consistency. Completed the TODO in the DiDi seed entry, enhancing the clarity of the code.

items.title: Enhance Connector Card Layout in Frontend Web 🎨
items.summary: This update improves the layout of connector cards by relocating Featured/New/Trending tags to the metadata row and implementing a three-column card header grid. Connector names can now wrap to two lines, enhancing readability. These changes aim to create a more stable and user-friendly interface.

items.title: Introducing a Local Web UI for Channels Configuration in Coding Bridge 🚀
items.summary: This update adds the `coding-bridge channels portal`, a local web UI that simplifies editing the `channels.toml` configuration file. Users can now search for contacts, select admins, and manage group settings without manually entering IDs. The portal operates securely on localhost and includes a robust testing suite to ensure reliability.

items.title: Enhance Service Creation Checklist in Index 🚀
items.summary: This update tightens the `create-service` workflow by requiring both `Service.icon_url` and `Service.thumbnail` to ensure high-quality product presentation. It also introduces documentation for asset usage, byte-range CDN URL verification, and mandates HTML landing pages for service introductions. Additionally, it addresses stale dependency graph numbering and outlines potential pitfalls for missing assets.

items.title: Optimize Maestro Worker Resources in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update right-sizes the maestro worker resources to improve scheduling on the cluster. Key changes include reducing the number of replicas from 6 to 4, lowering CPU requests from 2 to 500m, and cutting memory limits from 16Gi to 8Gi. These adjustments are based on live cluster data, ensuring efficient resource usage and preventing over-provisioning.

items.title: Introducing Wisdom Multi-Tenant Production on Azure AKS in Index 🚀
items.summary: This update adds the documentation for the Wisdom multi-tenant production deployment plan on Azure AKS, utilizing a golden-snapshot model. Key changes include the addition of a new plan file and an index row, which streamline tenant provisioning and significantly reduce setup time. The deployment strategy has been proven effective, ensuring a robust and efficient production environment.

items.title: Optimize Worker Configuration for Smooth Rollouts in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update right-sizes the worker configuration by reducing replicas from 6 to 4 and adjusting CPU requests from 2 to 1, ensuring successful deployments. Additionally, the deployment script now waits for the rollout to complete, preventing previous issues where outdated images were served. These changes enhance the reliability of the maestro deployment process.

items.title: Enhance MCP Boot Resilience in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update improves the local MCP boot process by extending the initialization timeout to 60 seconds and implementing a background retry mechanism for failed connections. These changes ensure that slow machines can recover from cold starts without user intervention, significantly enhancing user experience. Additionally, new tests have been added to validate these improvements.

items.title: Add Bluesky BYOC Connector in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces the `bluesky/bluesky` BYOC connector to the connector catalog, enabling users to publish posts to their Bluesky accounts using their handle and an App Password. The connector mirrors the existing `mastodon` BYOC connector and includes necessary credentials and configurations. This addition enhances the capabilities for developers and tech enthusiasts to engage with the Bluesky platform effectively.

items.title: Introducing Bluesky Skill for Seamless Posting in Skills 🚀
items.summary: This update adds a new Bluesky skill that allows users to publish, delete, and read their own posts via the AT Protocol. The skill includes capabilities for session creation, posting, and retrieving engagement stats, all while ensuring secure handling of user credentials. With this addition, users can now easily manage their Bluesky interactions directly from the platform.

items.title: Fix HappyHorse Deployment in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update adds the missing HappyHorse block to the `ttapi/deploy/run.sh` script, ensuring that the HappyHorse deployment and service are applied automatically during CI deployments. Previously, while the image was built, the deployment was skipped, requiring manual intervention. Now, future deployments will correctly include HappyHorse, streamlining the process.

items.title: Enhance Mastodon Integration with New Skill in Skills 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a new Mastodon skill that allows users to post toots, read their own stats, and manage their posts via the Mastodon REST API. Key functionalities include verifying user accounts, posting and deleting toots, and listing recent engagements. The skill is designed to facilitate content distribution on the fediverse, enhancing user interaction with their Mastodon instances.

items.title: Enhance Mastodon Integration with BYOC Connector in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a BYOC connector for Mastodon, allowing users to publish and delete toots while accessing their posts and engagement metrics. The connector requires two credentials: the instance URL and an access token, ensuring secure interactions with any Mastodon instance. The integration is designed to enhance content distribution for developers and marketers within the federated social network.

items.title: Enhance WeChat Quickstart with Provider Sign-In in Coding Bridge 🚀
items.summary: This update adds crucial documentation for signing in the provider (Claude Code/Codex) when using the WeChat channels quickstart. It clarifies the use of the `api.acedata.cloud` key and corrects inaccuracies in the README. The changes ensure users can effectively utilize the one-command setup for daily operations.

items.title: Display Task ID and Trace ID During Video Production in Nexior 🎥
items.summary: This update enhances the maestro task interface by displaying the Task ID and Trace ID while a video is still producing, rather than only after completion. The in-progress section now shows these IDs, allowing for easier tracing and support during long production runs. This change aligns the maestro's behavior with other components like veo and seedance, improving user experience.

items.title: Fix worker rollout and ensure live deployments in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update addresses critical issues with the maestro deployment process by resizing worker pods and ensuring that deployments wait for successful rollouts. The worker replicas have been adjusted from 6 to 4 with CPU requests reduced to 1, allowing for better scheduling and preventing deadlocks. Additionally, the deployment script now verifies that the new image is live on all pods before reporting success, enhancing reliability.

items.title: Exciting New Coze Plugin Specs Added in A P Is 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces six new Coze plugin specifications, enhancing our capabilities in image and video generation. The new plugins include Seedream, Flux, Sora, Kling, Veo, and Seedance, all designed for synchronous operations. Additionally, the README has been updated to reflect these changes and provide a clearer overview of our offerings.

items.title: Fix billing logic for no_content in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update addresses a billing issue identified during large-scale benchmarking, where certain pages were incorrectly billed due to misleading markdown content. The `resolveBlocked()` function now only considers visible text length for determining no_content status, ensuring that pages with excessive markdown do not trigger billing. Additionally, two new regression tests have been added to validate this change.

items.title: Introducing Substack BYOC Skill for Reading & Publishing in Skills 🚀
items.summary: This update adds the new Substack skill, enabling users to read and publish content on Substack using their own login cookies. The implementation leverages internal Substack endpoints, allowing for draft creation, validation, and publishing without an official write API. Key safety features include gated writes and controlled email notifications to subscribers.

items.title: Introducing TGStat Skill for Telegram Research in Skills 🚀
items.summary: This update adds the TGStat skill for Telegram channel research, enabling users to search channels by various criteria and retrieve audience engagement statistics. The skill integrates with the TGStat Stat API, providing essential data for marketing research in the Russian market. It includes validation checks to ensure compliance and accuracy with the API documentation.

items.title: Enhance Telegram Channel Research with New TGStat Connector in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a new BYOC API-key connector for TGStat, allowing users to search Telegram channels and retrieve audience engagement statistics. It integrates seamlessly with the existing AceDataCloud skills, enhancing marketing research capabilities specifically for the Russian-language market. The connector is designed for secure token usage and complies with relevant regulations.

items.title: Enhance HappyHorse Video APIs with Documentation and Pricing in Backend 🎥
items.summary: This update introduces the HappyHorse video integration to the PlatformBackend, including new API endpoints for videos and tasks. Key changes include the addition of OpenAPI specifications, service pricing rules, and comprehensive documentation in Chinese. The migration also ensures proper registration of the new service and its associated assets.

items.title: Exciting New HappyHorse Video Generation API in Platform Service 🎥
items.summary: This update introduces the HappyHorse video generation API to the ttapi worker, enabling new functionalities for video creation. A new public endpoint, `POST /happyhorse/videos`, supports various actions such as generating videos from prompts and converting images to videos. Key improvements include enhanced error handling and a dedicated Kubernetes pool for better resource management.

items.title: Retire PlatformPublisher and Enhance Marketing Channels in Index 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces batch 06 of the connector directory, which retires the PlatformPublisher in favor of aichat2 and connectors for content distribution. It also expands marketing channel coverage through three waves, targeting platforms like WordPress, Instagram, and 抖音. Documentation has been updated accordingly, ensuring clarity and consistency across the connector directory.

items.title: Empower Your Blog with Self-Hosted WordPress Skill in Skills 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a new skill for managing self-hosted WordPress sites using the WordPress REST API. Users can publish posts, manage categories, and upload media seamlessly with `curl + jq`. The skill ensures secure authentication and provides user-friendly error guidance, enhancing the blogging experience.

items.title: Enhance WordPress Connectivity with BYOC Connector in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a self-hosted WordPress BYOC connector, allowing users to connect their WordPress sites using Application Passwords for authentication. The new connector supports seamless publishing through the aichat2 skill, enhancing the integration capabilities for self-hosted WordPress users. Key validations ensure the connector's functionality and security are intact.

items.title: Enhance Hashnode Publishing Skill in Skills 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a new Hashnode publishing skill that allows users to publish, draft, update, and list posts directly from chat using the GraphQL API. The implementation follows the existing devto skill pattern, ensuring consistency in functionality and user experience. Additionally, a companion connector PR has been added to support the new skill.

items.title: Add Hashnode BYOC Connector in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces the Hashnode BYOC (bring-your-own-credentials) connector to the project, allowing users to publish articles directly to their Hashnode blogs using a Personal Access Token. The connector is fully integrated with the existing system, featuring a secure credential schema and auto-installs the necessary skills. The YAML configuration has been validated, ensuring no duplicate identifiers and maintaining a unique rank.

items.title: New Substack BYOC Connector Added in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a new Substack connector to the AuthBackend, enabling users to connect their Substack accounts and publish articles and newsletters seamlessly. The connector utilizes a cookie-based authentication method due to the absence of an official write API from Substack. Key configurations include the use of a browser extension for cookie capture, ensuring a smooth publishing flow.

items.title: Update autoprefixer to version 10.5.2 in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update bumps the autoprefixer dependency from version 10.5.1 to 10.5.2. Key changes include the adjustment of the order for the '-webkit-fill-available' and '-moz-available' properties, enhancing compatibility with Firefox. This ensures better adherence to CSS specifications and improves layout rendering across browsers.

items.title: Fix UTF-8 BOM Handling in Nexior <emoji>
items.summary: This update addresses a critical bug in the desktop Local Tools feature by stripping the UTF-8 BOM from the local-tools.json file during loading. Previously, a BOM could cause JSON parsing to fail, leading to silent data loss of authorized roots and MCP servers. The change ensures that configurations are loaded correctly, even if edited with tools that introduce a BOM, and includes new tests to validate this behavior.

items.title: Enhance Skill Installation Restrictions in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces hard-gate installation for skills based on surface restrictions, ensuring users can only install skills compatible with their platform. For instance, skills restricted to macOS will now display a locked state on incompatible platforms like Windows or Linux, along with informative tooltips. Additionally, internationalization support has been expanded to cover all 18 locales, enhancing user experience across different languages.

items.title: Exciting New Nano-Banana Models Added in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces three new public nano-banana model names to the BananaRouter worker, each served by a dedicated official channel pod. The models include `nano-banana:official`, `nano-banana-2:official`, and `nano-banana-pro:official`, all mapped to their respective native Gemini upstream IDs. Additionally, the deployment and service configurations have been updated to support these new models, enhancing the routing capabilities of the platform.

items.title: Add CHANGELOG for WeChat Channels Release in Coding Bridge 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces the first `CHANGELOG.md` for the CodingBridge repository, detailing the continuous release scheme and the WeChat chat channels milestone. Key features include an automated versioning system, a new WeChat channel adapter, and comprehensive documentation for various components. The changelog serves as a vital artifact for tracking user-facing changes and enhancements.

items.title: Enhance WeChat Chat Channels Documentation in Coding Bridge 🚀
items.summary: This update adds comprehensive documentation for the WeChat chat-channels feature in the README. Users can now easily discover and configure the feature, including setup instructions, verification commands, and safety measures. The documentation has been thoroughly reviewed for accuracy and clarity, ensuring a smooth user experience.

items.title: Exciting New Image Plugin for Coze in A P Is 🎨
items.summary: This update introduces a new image-generation plugin for Coze, supporting GPT Image, Nano Banana, and DALL·E through a single endpoint. The addition of `coze/image.yaml` allows users to generate images from text prompts seamlessly. The README has also been updated to reflect this new functionality and provide a roadmap for future enhancements.

items.title: Enhance Imagery Gate for Real Media in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update implements a deterministic imagery gate to ensure that projects labeled as 'succeeded' contain real sourced media, specifically for product and website scenarios. Key changes include directory pruning to improve accuracy and speed, and a new check that rejects projects lacking required imagery. The tests have been updated and passed successfully, ensuring robust functionality.

items.title: Enhance Skill Directory with Platform Badges in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: The skill directory now displays platform-specific badges for skills with defined surfaces, enhancing user experience. For instance, the 'apple-notes' skill shows a badge indicating it's available on macOS desktop. This change is purely client-side, ensuring users can browse skills without installation restrictions while setting clear expectations about platform compatibility.

items.title: Enhance apple-notes for macOS Desktop Only in Skills 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces the `surfaces: [mac]` declaration in the apple-notes skill, restricting its use to the macOS desktop environment. This change ensures that the skill is only runnable on the AceDataCloud desktop app, enhancing clarity for users. No backend changes were necessary, maintaining backward compatibility while allowing for future UI enhancements.

items.title: Prevent Billing for Empty Extractions in Platform Service 🚫
items.summary: This update introduces a new billing rule that prevents charges for genuinely empty extractions classified as 'general'. If the combined length of text and markdown is below a specified threshold, these extractions will now return a non-billable status. This change aims to enhance billing accuracy and ensure users are not charged for content that does not meet the minimum quality criteria.

items.title: Enhance Channel Observability with Structured Events in Coding Bridge 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces structured, privacy-safe observability for the CodingBridge channels stack, allowing operators to monitor channel traffic health without exposing message content. A new `TurnEvent` dataclass and `log_turn()` function have been added to emit detailed logs per turn, capturing essential metrics while ensuring sensitive information remains secure. The implementation includes robust error handling and comprehensive tests to validate functionality and prevent data leaks.

items.title: New Service Deployment Templates for Coding Bridge in Coding Bridge 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces comprehensive documentation for deploying the `coding-bridge channels start` command as a background service across various operating systems. Templates for systemd (Linux), Task Scheduler/NSSM (Windows), and launchd (macOS) are now available, ensuring the service remains active through logouts and reboots. Key correctness points have been verified, enhancing the reliability and security of the deployment process.

items.title: Align Logging for Task Responses in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update moves the logging of 'updated task response' into the appropriate guard to ensure it only logs when a database write occurs. This change addresses a logging inconsistency that could mislead during error investigations, although it has no functional impact. The adjustment aligns the logging behavior with similar implementations across other services.

items.title: Enhance Provider Validation and Routing in Coding Bridge 🚀
items.summary: This update improves provider selection by ensuring that the `default_provider` is validated against known providers at load time, preventing silent fallbacks to the default provider. Key changes include enhanced error handling in `config.py` and validation in the CLI commands. The tests have been updated to ensure robust coverage for these changes.

items.title: Enhance Channels with E2E Smoke Testing in Coding Bridge 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a comprehensive end-to-end smoke test for the channels feature, ensuring its reliability. Key additions include an offline integration test that verifies the entire message flow and a CLI command for local provider checks. These enhancements will improve the robustness of the channels setup and facilitate smoother deployments.

items.title: Launch coding-bridge channels CLI commands in Coding Bridge 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces the `coding-bridge channels` subcommand group, enabling users to initialize, start, and validate WeChat bot configurations. Key commands include `init` for creating a configuration file, `start` for running the bot, and `doctor` for validating tokens without exposing sensitive information. The changes enhance the CLI functionality while ensuring safety and reliability in configuration management.

items.title: Enhance Asset-First Routing for Real Subjects in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update refines the maestro quality fix by ensuring that real subjects are paired with actual assets in video workflows. Key changes include rewording the scenario directive to prioritize fidelity and introducing new routing rules that prevent the use of invented visuals for real subjects. The README and architecture documents have been updated to reflect these changes, enhancing clarity and guidance for users.

items.title: Enhance Maestro's Preflight Checks in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update enforces a hard-fail for silent renders in the maestro worker's preflight checks, ensuring that videos without audio are rejected. The change introduces a new `require_audio` flag, making it mandatory for most scenarios to have sound, while only allowing motion graphics to be silent. This improvement prevents mute videos from being incorrectly marked as successful, enhancing overall video quality.

items.title: Transform Wisdom Appliance into a Turnkey Solution in Wisdom 🚀
items.summary: This update enhances the dockur Windows Wisdom appliance, making it fully turnkey. Key improvements include automatic installation of necessary components on first boot, enabling access to the Wisdom API at port 8000, and ensuring WeChat launches seamlessly. Multiple bugs were addressed, significantly improving the user experience and reliability of the installation process.

items.title: Enhance Abuse Controls for WeChat Bot in Coding Bridge 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces robust abuse-control mechanisms for the WeChat bot, including a trigger prefix, sender allowlist, per-sender rate limits, and message ID deduplication. New tests have been added to ensure the reliability of these features, enhancing the bot's security and performance. Additionally, a helper function for task status retrieval has been implemented to streamline operations.

items.title: Enhance WeChat Channel Config with TOML Schema in Coding Bridge 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a TOML configuration schema for WeChat channel adapters, allowing for flexible and safe configuration management. Key additions include a new `WeChatInstanceConfig` class and a robust error handling mechanism for configuration loading. Additionally, 34 new tests ensure the reliability of the new features.

items.title: Introducing WeChat Adapter for Enhanced Messaging in Coding Bridge 🚀
items.summary: This update adds a fully functional WeChat `ChannelAdapter` that implements the P1 Protocol, allowing for seamless integration with WeChat's messaging system. Key features include an async HTTP client, structured message filtering, and robust error handling with token redaction. The implementation is backed by extensive tests to ensure reliability and performance.

items.title: Enhance SSRF Guard for Sub-Frames in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update improves the SSRF guard mechanism by ensuring that failures in sub-frames do not disrupt the main frame rendering process. The main frame now accurately reports DNS errors without misleading messages, enhancing error handling. Additionally, the changes include new tests for reason-code coverage, ensuring robust validation across various scenarios.

items.title: Fix documentation for aspect ratio and resolution in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update corrects the documentation regarding the behavior of aspect ratio and resolution parameters during the edit mode for nano-banana. The previous descriptions inaccurately stated that these parameters only applied during generation, while they actually take effect when provided in edit mode as well. The changes ensure that users have accurate guidance on how to use these parameters effectively.

items.title: Optimize Image Handling for GPT-Image-2 Edits in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update addresses the issue of oversized input images causing failures in the async `/images/edits` endpoint for the `gpt-image-2:official` model. The solution involves downscaling images exceeding the 1024x1024 pixel limit while preserving their aspect ratio and maximizing resolution. Additionally, the implementation introduces the `sharp` library for efficient image processing, ensuring a seamless user experience.

items.title: New Apple Notes Skill for macOS in Skills 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a new **apple-notes** Agent Skill, enabling users to manage Apple Notes on macOS through AppleScript. Key functionalities include listing, searching, reading, creating, appending, moving, and deleting notes, all while ensuring local operation without a cloud API. The skill is designed with safety features like GATED writes to prevent accidental data loss.

items.title: Exciting New DiDi BYOC Connector Added in Backend 🚀
items.summary: We've introduced the DiDi BYOC connector, allowing users to connect their DiDi MCP key seamlessly. This addition enhances automation capabilities and auto-installs the necessary skills for a smooth user experience. Remember to run the sync command post-merge to update the connector manifest!

items.title: Enhance Ride-Hailing with DiDi Skill in Skills 🚖
items.summary: This update introduces a new DiDi ride-hailing skill that allows users to book rides, estimate fares, and manage orders through the DiDi MCP server. Key files include detailed skill instructions, a Streamable-HTTP client for communication, and a comprehensive API reference. The implementation ensures safety with write-gating for sensitive operations, enhancing user security.

items.title: Introducing Tencent Docs BYOC Connector in Backend 🚀
items.summary: We've added a new BYOC connector for Tencent Docs to our AuthBackend, enhancing our productivity tool offerings. This connector allows users to manage their Tencent Docs directly through our platform, supporting OAuth2 authentication and providing a seamless experience. The integration includes essential credential management and localized suggestions for improved user interaction.

items.title: Introducing Tencent Docs BYOC Connector Skill in Skills 🚀
items.summary: We've added a new Tencent Docs BYOC connector skill that allows users to create, read, list, search, and manage their documents, sheets, and more within their Tencent Docs space. This skill integrates seamlessly with the Tencent Docs Open API and follows the same structure as our existing connector skills. Key features include authentication handling and various document management recipes to enhance user experience.

items.title: Revamp MCP Server Fields for Better Layout in Nexior 🎨
items.summary: The Local Tools MCP server editor has been polished with a new two-column form grid layout, enhancing the alignment and readability of server fields. Key changes include replacing cramped inputs with default-sized fields and improving label visibility. This update ensures a cleaner user interface without altering any existing functionality.

items.title: Enhance Medium Integration with Clickable Links and Tables in Skills 🚀
items.summary: This update improves the Medium integration by fixing rendering issues and adding new features. Key changes include the implementation of clickable links, proper table rendering, and inline markup support. Additionally, the xsrf token handling has been enhanced to prevent errors during state-changing operations.

items.title: Introducing WeCom BYOC Connector in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update adds a WeCom connector to the seed directory, enabling users to authenticate using their own credentials through the BYOC method. The connector supports essential operations like reading contacts, sending messages, and managing schedules within WeCom. Credentials are securely encrypted and injected only during skill execution, ensuring data safety.

items.title: Introducing WeCom Connector Skill for Enhanced Communication in Skills 🚀
items.summary: This update adds a new WeCom (企业微信) connector skill, enabling agents to interact with WeCom's server-side API through a self-built app. Key features include managing contacts, sending app messages, creating documents, and scheduling meetings. The skill is designed to streamline operations for users leveraging WeCom's functionalities.

items.title: Enhance multipart field handling in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update fixes an issue where multiple uploaded images were not being stored correctly in the task record, leading to only one image being displayed. The `decodeRequestBody()` function now accumulates repeated field names into arrays, ensuring all images are preserved. This change enhances the reliability of multi-image edits in the Nexior chat history.

items.title: Enhance Task Handling to Prevent Intermediate Errors in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a safeguard in the task handling process across multiple channels to prevent erroneous writes to the task store during failover scenarios. Specifically, it adds a condition to skip writing failure results when in `routerManaged` mode, ensuring that clients do not see intermediate error states. This change enhances the reliability of the image generation process reported by users.

items.title: Introducing the Coze Plugin for AceData Cloud in A P Is 🎶
items.summary: We've launched the first Coze plugin for AceData Cloud, enabling seamless integration of our AI music generation capabilities. This plugin utilizes a standard OpenAPI 3.0 schema, allowing Coze users to generate music through a simple API call. The accompanying README provides a comprehensive guide for importing and utilizing the plugin effectively.

items.title: Fix Local Tools Panel Rendering Issue in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update resolves a critical issue where the Local Tools settings panel was rendering blank across all languages due to a bare '@' in the translation string. By escaping the '@' using vue-i18n's literal interpolation, the panel now displays correctly in all 18 locales. Comprehensive tests have been added to ensure the fix is robust and prevents future regressions.

items.title: Enhance Channel Adapter and Session Dispatcher in Coding Bridge 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces an additive scaffolding for the channels stack, including a new `ChannelAdapter` protocol and a `SessionDispatcher` that manages message sessions. Key changes include the addition of new value objects and comprehensive tests to ensure protocol conformance and error handling. The implementation maintains existing functionality while laying the groundwork for future enhancements.

items.title: Fix SSR Rendering Issues for Dynamic Content in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update addresses a critical issue where server-side rendered pages returned an empty shell due to unguarded access to browser globals. Key changes include adding checks for `window` and `navigator` in the utility functions to ensure proper rendering during SSR. As a result, all affected routes now return full HTML content, improving SEO and user experience.

items.title: Fix assumptions and add production plan in Index 🚀
items.summary: This PR corrects two incorrect assumptions from the previous merge regarding the `WisdomChannel` and DNS configurations. It introduces a new production plan specifically for the `coding-bridge` development tool path, detailing critical dependencies and sequential PRs necessary for implementation. Additionally, updates to the README and existing planning documents ensure clarity and alignment with the latest project scope.

items.title: Final Cleanup of Dead Code and Docs in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This PR completes the cleanup process initiated by the WebExtrator hardening audit. It removes unused code, including the `sync-guard.ts` file and the `hits` field from `CacheEnvelope`, which was never utilized. Additionally, the `.env.example` file has been fully synchronized with the current environment variables, ensuring all values are documented and match the schema defaults.

items.title: Enhance Reliability and Infrastructure in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update focuses on improving the reliability and infrastructure of the WebExtrator. Key changes include the separation of Redis instances for caching and queue management, implementation of graceful shutdown procedures, and enhancements to Horizontal Pod Autoscaler responsiveness. These adjustments aim to ensure job durability and optimize resource management.

items.title: Enhance WebExtrator with Cache Improvements in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces significant enhancements to the WebExtrator, focusing on caching mechanisms. Key changes include implementing in-process single-flight caching to reduce redundant renders, introducing a best-effort job cancellation on sync timeouts, and enforcing a size cap on cached results to optimize memory usage. These improvements aim to enhance performance and reliability in handling concurrent requests.

items.title: Sync Settings Nav Highlight with Active Tab in Nexior 🎨
items.summary: This update addresses two key issues in the desktop Settings dialog: the left navigation highlight was desynchronized from the displayed content, and a blank content pane appeared under certain conditions. The solution involves creating a computed property to ensure the highlight and content are always in sync, preventing any blank displays. The changes enhance user experience by providing consistent navigation feedback.

items.title: Enhance Billing and Extraction Quality in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update addresses three key audit findings in the WebExtrator component of the Platform Service. It introduces a new error classification system, improves billing accuracy by adjusting how blocked statuses are handled, and enhances content type detection to ensure better extraction quality. These changes aim to streamline operations and improve user experience.

items.title: Enhance TTS and Graphics Handling in Platform Service 🎥
items.summary: This update addresses critical issues in the Maestro video task related to TTS and graphics rendering. Key changes include pinning the synthesis call to prevent endpoint guessing and adding a mandatory overlays delivery gate to ensure graphics are correctly rendered. These improvements enhance the reliability of video outputs and streamline the processing workflow.

items.title: Exciting New CodingBridge Channels Plan Launched in Index 🚀
items.summary: The new CodingBridge Channels plan introduces an end-to-end proof of concept (POC) and architecture aimed at integrating various messaging channels like WeChat, Telegram, and Discord with CodingBridge. Key updates include the successful execution of the POC, which connects WeChat to the CodingBridge agent, and the consolidation of previous implementations into a streamlined architecture. This plan sets the stage for future enhancements and integrations across multiple platforms.

items.title: Fix 24h Traffic KPI Stuck at Sample Cap in Backend 🚀
items.summary: The 24h traffic KPI on the superuser dashboard was previously fixed at 10,000 due to a sampling limit. This update implements an exact database aggregate for request and success counts, ensuring the KPI reflects real traffic over time. The sampling limit has been renamed to clarify its purpose for latency estimation, while the overall functionality remains intact.

items.title: Strengthening WebExtrator Security in Platform Service 🔒
items.summary: This update enhances the security of WebExtrator by addressing three critical issues: SSRF vulnerabilities, error message sanitization, and enforcing TLS validation for callbacks. Key changes include the introduction of a robust SSRF guard that validates URLs against private and internal addresses, improved error handling to prevent sensitive information leakage, and mandatory TLS certificate verification for callback deliveries. These enhancements significantly bolster the overall security posture of the Platform Service.

items.title: Enhance Mock Tests for Python 3.10 Compatibility in Wisdom 🚀
items.summary: This update improves the portability of mock tests to ensure compatibility with Python 3.10, addressing 15 failing tests. Key changes include using `patch.object` to avoid function/submodule name shadowing and implementing a deterministic fallback for the PrintWindow test across different operating systems. The modifications enhance test reliability without impacting the product runtime.

items.title: Fix typo in insufficient balance error message in Gateway 🚀
items.summary: This update corrects a typo in the 403 error message related to insufficient balance in the PlatformGateway. The URL was changed from 'https://platfrom.acedata.cloud' to 'https://platform.acedata.cloud', ensuring users receive the correct link to top up their balance. This change does not affect any logic or error codes.

items.title: WebExtrator Hardening Audit Completed in Index 🚀
items.summary: A comprehensive hardening audit of the WebExtrator service has been conducted, identifying 16 issues through source code review and real-world testing. This PR documents the findings and outlines a remediation plan that includes five subsequent PRs to address the identified vulnerabilities. Key issues include SSRF vulnerabilities, response size limits, and error handling improvements.

items.title: Fix header encoding to prevent crashes in Wisdom 🚀
items.summary: This update percent-encodes the `X-Media-Path` response header to handle non-ASCII characters, preventing crashes when serving media files. The change ensures that HTTP headers remain compliant with latin-1 encoding, thus avoiding `UnicodeEncodeError` issues. Testing has confirmed that the media-serving endpoint operates smoothly post-deployment.

items.title: Fix double charging issue by billing on completion in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update resolves a double charging issue in the maestro video submission process. By marking the create response with `X-Usage-Exempt`, the system ensures that charges occur only after successful video production, preventing premature deductions. The fix has been tested and verified, maintaining the integrity of the billing monitor as the sole biller.

items.title: Update FontAwesome Vue Package to Latest Version in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update bumps the @fortawesome/vue-fontawesome package from version 3.2.0 to 3.3.0. Key enhancements include support for new animation properties introduced in Font Awesome 7.3.0 and improved TypeScript types for icon and mask props, ensuring better performance and usability.

items.title: Enhance Streaming Export Performance in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update resolves timeout issues for large CSV exports by reintroducing `StreamingHttpResponse` and allowing synchronous ORM calls in an async context. The first byte is now sent immediately, preventing client and proxy timeouts, while memory usage remains efficient with 2000 rows per batch. The fix preserves existing filters and the 1M row cap, ensuring a smooth user experience.
